audit_items.proof_trace: `GetPixelWriteMask` is declared `static inline Quantum` and returns a numeric value (`Quantum` is typedef'd to unsigned char/short/int or float depending on build depth) — never a pointer. The function either returns `(Quantum) QuantumRange` (when WriteMaskPixelChannel is undefined) or an integer pixel value; NULL is not a valid member of this type. Every caller across all 22 files uses the return value in an arithmetic comparison (`<= (QuantumRange/2)`), with no pointer dereference anywhere.
